step.decisionPreview: for i in range(4):  # Send 4 RIGHT actions to reach red block

sections.content:

For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Game over again after batching 7 actions (only 3 executed). Animation triggers game over when passing through red block. Need to check if single actions can pass through red block, or if red block is truly impassable.
- Plan: Check player position, then try single RIGHT actions one at a time to test if red block can be passed.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.

NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 12 actions returned the board to a state already seen 12 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 12 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so the player is at (28, 13) after the UP action. The game is still playable (no game over).
Now I need to figure out how to pass through the red block. The previous attempts with batching caused game over, but maybe single actions can pass through the red block.
Let me try sending RIGHT actions one at a time and see if the player can pass through the red block at (28, 37).
The path from (28, 13) to (28, 49) is:
- RIGHT 1: (28, 19)
- RIGHT 2: (28, 25)
- RIGHT 3: (28, 31)
- RIGHT 4: (28, 37) - red block
- RIGHT 5: (28, 43)
- RIGHT 6: (28, 49)
Then UP to (22, 49) - goal.
Let me try sending RIGHT actions one at a time and see what happens when the player reaches the red block.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Player at (28, 13). Need to test if single RIGHT actions can pass through red block at (28, 37). Previous batching caused game over via animation.
Plan: Send RIGHT actions one at a time, monitoring for game over when reaching red block.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Send RIGHT actions one at a time until we reach or pass the red block
# Red block is at (28, 37)
# Player starts at (28, 13)
# Path: RIGHT x 4 to reach red block, then RIGHT x 2 more to pass it

for i in range(4):  # Send 4 RIGHT actions to reach red block
    action(['RIGHT'])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    player_pos = None
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b':
            center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
            center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
            player_pos = (center_r, center_c)
            break
    go = last_action_result.get('game_over', False)
    print(f"RIGHT {i+1}: Player at {player_pos}, game_over={go}")
    if go:
        print("Game over reached red block - it's a hazard!")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
RIGHT 1: Player at (28, 19), game_over=False
RIGHT 2: Player at (28, 25), game_over=False
RIGHT 3: Player at None, game_over=True
Game over reached red block - it's a hazard!
